evidence.scientific_clarity: All brief requirements met: 12 study CI lines with square markers, red diamond for pooled fixed-effects estimate, dotted null-effect vertical line, and right-side numerical annotations.
evidence.typography: Default DejaVu Sans used throughout; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap) fonts in PDF — script never sets pdf.fonttype=42 or rcParams font family.
evidence.palette: Colorblind-safe palette: blue (#1f77b4) for individual study squares, red (#d62728) for pooled diamond, dark grey for CI lines; no green/red directional confusion.
evidence.layout_density: Layout is functional but pooled estimate label is duplicated (y-axis tick AND right annotation column); x-axis extended asymmetrically, leaving slight right-heavy imbalance.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content; pooled estimate is numerically explicit; however, no heterogeneity statistic (I²/Q) is reported, and the duplicate pooled label on y-tick plus annotation column is redundant.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF fonts are Type 3 (custom-encoded bitmaps) per pdffonts output; text is not selectable or editable — script lacks matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype'] = 42.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (34 KB), figure.png (86 KB), caption.tex (428 B), gen_figure.py (3.7 KB); bonus figure.svg also generated.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption clearly explains squares=point estimates, lines=95% CI, red diamond=pooled IV-FE estimate; reports specific numerical finding θ_pool=0.245 [0.196, 0.294] and defines the null-effect reference line.

overall_strengths: Complete, readable forest plot that satisfies all brief requirements: per-study CI lines, diamond pooled estimate, and null-effect reference line.
Caption is informative and scientifically precise, providing the pooled estimate value, CI, method (inverse-variance fixed-effects), and a description of every graphical element.
overall_weaknesses: PDF fonts are Type 3 (bitmap) rather than Type 1/TrueType — script must set pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' for editable, selectable text in vector outputs.
Default DejaVu Sans font family retained; no heterogeneity statistics (I², Q-test) reported in caption or figure, which Reviewer 2 would almost certainly request for a meta-analysis.
